metadata.title: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men
metadata.study_design: Comparative study of healthy, lean young men and women during prolonged overnight fast using intravenous infusion of [U-13C]palmitate
metadata.demographics: Healthy, lean young men and women (n=12) matched for age and BMI; women studied during follicular phase of menstrual cycle

evidence.quote: British Journal of Nutrition (2011), 105, 857–865
Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men
Kyriakoula Marinou, Martin Adiels, Leanne Hodson, Keith N. Frayn, Fredrik Karpe and Barbara A. Fielding

The present study aimed to investigate the utilisation of plasma NEFA by the liver and to determine whether they are partitioned differently into ketone bodies and VLDL-TAG in healthy, lean young men and women. Volunteers were studied during a prolonged overnight fast (12–19h) using an intravenous infusion of [U-13C]palmitate.
Healthy, lean young men and women (n 12) were studied, and matched for age and BMI
The studies were performed during the first week (follicular phase) of the menstrual cycle
Healthy, lean young men and women (n 12) were studied
in exact accordance with the six men and six women in the present study
Age (years) 25·5 4·4
Age (years) 22·5 2·2
All individuals were selected to have a BMI of less than 25 kg/m2

methods_participants.label: Inclusion criterion: health status
methods_participants.value: Healthy, lean, weight stable, no disease, no lipid-lowering medication

evidence.quote: Subjects, recruited from the wider Oxford community via advertisement, were free from any disease, weight stable and not taking any lipid-lowering medication or medication that would alter lipid metabolism
Before the study day, subjects were asked to avoid foodstuffs naturally enriched in 13C for 48h

methods_participants.label: Study timing: exercise and alcohol avoidance

evidence.quote: refrain from strenuous exercise and alcohol for 24h
After 12h fasting, the men had a less advantageous metabolic profile
This was continued until the end of the experiment (at 7h)
Fat mass was measured by bioelectrical impedance analysis

At time 0, a continuous intravenous infusion (0·04 mmol/kg per min) of potassium [U-13C]palmitate (isotope purity 98%; Cambridge Isotope Laboratories, Inc., Andover, MA, USA) complexed to human albumin was started
a continuous intravenous infusion (0·04 mmol/kg per min) of potassium [U-13C]palmitate
plasma NEFA, VLDL-TAG and 3-OHB concentrations were determined enzymatically
Plasma insulin concentrations were measured by RIA

methods_participants.label: Assay: VLDL isolation
methods_participants.value: sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation

evidence.quote: VLDL (Svedberg flotation rate (Sf) 60–400) and VLDL (Sf 20–60) were isolated by sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation
isotopic enrichment was measured by GC and GC–MS, respectively
The studies were performed during the first week (follicular phase) of the menstrual cycle

narrative_results.statement: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men

evidence.quote: The ability of young women to partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG may contribute to their more advantageous metabolic profile compared with young men.

narrative_results.statement: After 12h fasting, women had a more advantageous metabolic profile with lower plasma glucose and TAG but higher plasma NEFA concentrations

evidence.quote: After 12h fasting, the women had a more advantageous metabolic profile with lower plasma glucose (P<0·05) and TAG (P<0·05) but higher plasma NEFA (P<0·05) concentrations.

narrative_results.statement: Plasma 3-hydroxybutyrate concentrations rose more in women than in men

evidence.quote: Plasma 3-hydroxybutyrate (3-OHB) concentrations rose more in women than in men

narrative_results.statement: The transfer of 13C from [U-13C]palmitate to plasma [13C]3-OHB reached a plateau 6–7h after the start of the infusion in women but was still increasing at 6h in men

evidence.quote: the transfer of 13C from [U-13C]palmitate to plasma [13C]3-OHB reached a plateau 6–7h after the start of the infusion in women but was still increasing at 6h in men.

narrative_results.statement: In women, systemic plasma fatty acids were the major source of 3-OHB production, but in men, this was not observed

evidence.quote: In women, the high isotopic enrichment of plasma 3-OHB suggested that systemic plasma fatty acids were the major source of 3-OHB production. However, in men, this was not observed during the course of the study (P<0·01).

narrative_results.statement: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL-TAG

evidence.quote: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL - or VLDL -TAG.

narrative_results.statement: The ratio of [13C]3-OHB:[13C]VLDL-TAG in the plasma was significantly higher in women than in men

evidence.quote: The ratio of [13C]3-OHB:[13C]VLDL-TAG in the plasma was significantly higher in women than in men (2·73 (SE 1·7) v. 0·66 (SE 0·30); P¼0·027 for VLDL1 and 3·24 (SE 1·6) v. 0·97 (SE 0·43); P¼0·014 for VLDL2).

In women, the observed isotopic enrichment of the fatty acid tracer in 3-OHB was close to that predicted if systemic plasma fatty acids were the sole source of 3-OHB production
In men, the observed enrichment was less than half expected, implying either an alternative source of fatty acids or a slower rate of production

semantic_groups.rationale: Pass 1 provides specific counts (6 men, 6 women); Pass 2 only states both sexes present. Same underlying fact, different specificity levels.
Pass 1 shows only men's age; Pass 2 shows both men (25.5) and women (22.5). Pass 1 is incomplete but not contradictory.
Pass 1 claims to report women's age (22.5 years) but Pass 2 reports BMI data instead. These are different facts entirely.
Pass 1 contains BMI inclusion criterion; Pass 2 is empty. Orphan entry from Pass 1.
Both describe identical health status inclusion criteria with negligible wording differences (criterion vs criteria).
Both report 48-hour food avoidance pre-study. Pass 2 adds detail about 13C enrichment but core fact is identical.
Both report 24-hour exercise and alcohol avoidance. Pass 2 adds clarification about strenuous exercise but core fact is identical.
Pass 1 specifies 12 hours; Pass 2 states 'overnight fast' without hours. Same fact, different expression of duration.
Both describe 7-hour duration; pass_1 emphasizes total experiment duration while pass_2 specifies infusion duration, but both convey the same temporal fact
Identical information about bioelectrical impedance analysis for body composition measurement; only minor labeling difference
Identical descriptions of isotope tracer infusion method, substance, and units
Both describe 0.04 mmol/kg per min infusion rate via continuous IV; pass_1 specifies '[U-13C]palmitate' while pass_2 uses 'isotope tracer dose' but refer to same measurement
Both describe enzymatic determination for plasma metabolite analysis; pass_2 provides more specific metabolite list (NEFA, VLDL-TAG, 3-OHB) but same assay method
Identical information about RIA method for plasma insulin measurement; only minor wording variation
Both describe density gradient ultracentrifugation for VLDL isolation; pass_2 adds specific Sf fractions (VLDL1 and VLDL2) providing additional detail but same core method
Identical information about GC and GC-MS for fatty acid analysis
Both describe follicular phase timing; only punctuation differs (colon vs dash)
Identical participant count of 12
Pre-study meal information only in pass_2; missing from pass_1
Isotope tracer purity information only in pass_2; missing from pass_1
Pass 1 uses 'more advantageous metabolic profile' as framing while Pass 2 directly states the comparison with men. Both convey identical factual content about glucose, TAG, and NEFA levels.
Both extractions are identical in wording and content.
Pass 1 includes isotopic notation '[U-13C]palmitate' and '[13C]3-OHB' while Pass 2 simplifies to 'palmitate' and '3-OHB'. The factual content about timing and sex differences is identical.
Both extractions are identical in wording and content.
Both extractions are identical in wording and content.
Negligible difference: Pass 1 includes 'the' before 'plasma' while Pass 2 omits it. Content is identical.
Both extractions are identical in wording and content.
Pass 1 is empty while Pass 2 contains a valid extraction about isotopic enrichment in women. This is an orphan statement from Pass 2.
Pass 1 is empty while Pass 2 contains a specific finding about fatty acid enrichment in men. No comparison possible.
